explanation_used:
1. **Opening statement**: This neuron strongly responds to **text that presents evidence, examples, or demonstrations** (such as phrases containing “shown,” “demonstrated,” “exemplified,” “demo,” or references to protests/exhibitions as forms of demonstration).

2. **Domain coverage**: The pattern appears across diverse domains including public health, politics, sports, technology, business, and legal contexts, in English and other languages (e.g., German “Demonstration”). It activates on explicit mentions of showing proof (“have shown little sign”), illustrative cases (“best exemplified by”), trial versions (“demo account”), and public protests (“mass demonstrations”).

3. **Contrast with low-activation examples**: The low-activation texts are typically headlines, datelines, or sentence fragments lacking any presentation of evidence or examples. They contain no verbs or constructions indicating demonstration, proof, or illustration (e.g., simple announcements, incomplete phrases, or neutral statements without illustrative intent).

4. **Closing interpretation**: The neuron detects linguistic constructions where something is being presented as evidence, an instance, or a visible manifestation. It functions as a semantic marker for exemplification and verification across topics and languages.

results.actual_text: and Urban-Rural Development and Wujin Green Building Industrial Agglomeration Demonstration Zone. Dedicated to green development, LVHE Environmental Technology Co., Ltd.
results.plain_text: Helsinki, Finland — ZenRobotics Ltd. and Jiangsu LVHE Environmental Technology Co., Ltd. have agreed on cooperation on the first robotic construction and demolition waste recycling facility in China. The first units will be installed in summer 2017. The companies have also signed an exclusive partnership for the distribution of ZenRobotics Recycler, the first commercially available robotic waste sorting system, in China.  Jiangsu LVHE Environmental Technology Co., Ltd. is a state-owned holding enterprise under the Ministry of Housing and Urban-Rural Development and Wujin Green Building Industrial Agglomeration Demonstration Zone. Dedicated to green development, LVHE Environmental Technology Co., Ltd. develops sustainable resource utilization and environmental protection. The company has become the forerunner in C&D waste recycling in China.  “We keep exploring and innovating in C&D waste recycling in order to develop the green building industry. However, it is very difficult to dispose the mixed C&D waste in China because of the massive quantities and multiple waste categories. Now we can solve the problems. ZenRobotics’ technology is a great addition to our operations as we aim to employ the latest innovations, newest processes and modern equipment in producing new sustainable building materials”, explains Xue Feng, LVHE General Manager.  Robotic waste sorting has huge potential in China where the volume of construction waste has risen rapidly in line with the accelerating urbanization. Modern technological solutions are on demand especially in China’s Eco-cities.  “We’re very excited about our cooperation with Jiangsu LVHE Environmental Technology Co., Ltd. and our entry to China. There are huge opportunities on the Chinese market as the volume of construction waste is mounting up. High-quality fractions and high cost-efficiency are key benefits of robotic waste sorting”, commented ZenRobotics CEO Timo Taalas.  Source: ZenRobotics Ltd.

Dedicated to green development, LVHE Environmental Technology Co., Ltd. develops sustainable resource utilization and environmental protection.

results.actual_text: criteria: • Enrolled as an undergraduate student in an accredited nursing program. • Demonstrated commitment to pursuing a career in nursing. • Academic excellence, as evidenced by
results.plain_text:
Transforming Futures: The Dr. John Pispidikis Scholarship for Nursing Launches to Support Aspiring Nurses Nationwide  
    EINPresswire.com/ -- The healthcare industry is set to witness a transformative initiative with the launch of the prestigious Dr. John Pispidikis Scholarship for Nursing. Aimed at fostering the next generation of nursing professionals, this scholarship represents a significant opportunity for undergraduate students across the United States who demonstrates academic excellence, leadership, and a deep commitment to the nursing profession. 
 Dr. John Pispidikis, NP-C, DC, DACNB, DAAPM, AADEP, the distinguished healthcare professional behind this scholarship, has dedicated his career to improving patient care and supporting future healthcare leaders. The John Pispidikis Scholarship for Nursing offers a one-time award of $1,000 to an exemplary nursing student, providing essential financial support to help them achieve their educational aspirations. 
 Scholarship Criteria and Application Details To be eligible for the Dr. John Pispidikis Scholarship for Nursing, applicants must meet the following criteria: • Enrolled as an undergraduate student in an accredited nursing program. • Demonstrated commitment to pursuing a career in nursing. • Academic excellence, as evidenced by a minimum GPA of 3.0 on a 4.0 scale. • Strong leadership skills and involvement in extracurricular activities or community service. 
 Applicants are required to submit an essay of 500 words or less, describing a moment or experience that inspired them to pursue a career in nursing and how they envision making a difference in the field. All submissions, along with the applicant's name, contact information, and proof of enrollment, should be emailed to apply@johnpispidikisscholarship.com by April 15, 2025. 
 The recipient of the scholarship will be announced on May 15, 2025. 
 About Dr. John Pispidikis Dr. John Pispidikis’s career is marked by a series of notable achievements and a commitment to excellence in healthcare. He currently serves as the Chiropractor/Nurse Practitioner and Administrator at the Spinal Recovery Center in Warren, MI, where he has been a pivotal figure since 2014. Dr. Pispidikis's professional journey began in Texas, where he held numerous leadership roles, including Clinic Director at various healthcare systems. 
 His academic background is equally impressive, with degrees in economics from the University of Western Ontario and the University of Windsor, a Doctorate of Chiropractic from Parker College of Chiropractic, and advanced nursing degrees from Hardin-Simmons University and Samford University. Throughout his career, Dr. John Pispidikis has accumulated a myriad of board certifications and specialized training, underscoring his unwavering commitment to continuous learning and exceptional patient care. 
 Dr. John Pispidikis has been honored with several accolades, including the prestigious Best Chiropractor award from Hour Magazine and the Best of Warren Award in the Chiropractic Clinics category. Beyond his professional accomplishments, Dr. John Pispidikis is deeply involved in community service, volunteering with organizations such as South Oakland Shelter and Project Night Night. 
 The Dr. John Pispidikis Scholarship for Nursing is a testament to Dr. John Pispidikis's dedication to fostering the next generation of healthcare professionals. By offering this scholarship, Dr. John Pispidikis aims to support aspiring nurses in their educational journeys, helping them make significant contributions to the healthcare field. 
 About the John Pispidikis Scholarship for Nursing The Dr. John Pispidikis Scholarship for Nursing is an initiative designed to provide financial support to undergraduate students enrolled in accredited nursing programs across the United States. Established by Dr. John Pispidikis, NP-C, DC, DACNB, DAAPM, AADEP, the scholarship seeks to recognize and reward students who exhibit academic excellence, leadership, and a strong commitment to the nursing profession. The scholarship offers a one-time award of $1,000, with the goal of helping aspiring nurses achieve their educational and career goals. 
 For more information about the scholarship and how to apply, please visit http://drjohnpispidikisscholarship.com/.

results.actual_text: The Bumbershoot Music & Arts Festival will return in 2022
results.plain_text: The Bumbershoot Music & Arts Festival will return in 2022, organizers say, and when it does, it will be very different. Seattle Center is using the two-year pandemic pause to reimagine the 50-year-old Seattle mainstay and chart a new future without One Reel Productions, the company that has run the festival for more than a quarter of a century. “Although we’ll have some say in things, helping select the new producer and things like that, One Reel is pretty much effectively out of the Bumbershoot business,” said Marty Griswold, One Reel’s executive director. “It’s a seismic change basically.” One Reel has been involved with Bumbershoot in some way since 1972. The company assumed programming responsibilities in 1995 and it grew the annual three-day Labor Day weekend event into one of the nation’s premier summer festivals. Bumbershoot has struggled financially in recent years, however, and Seattle Center officials and community leaders believe that under a production deal that gave concert giant AEG programming rights, Bumbershoot overemphasized the music festival component in recent years and strayed from its original mission to showcase and celebrate artistic and cultural diversity within the community. So officials will soon form a committee that includes Robert Nellams, Seattle Center director; Marc Jones, the Seattle Center’s director of marketing and business development; One Reel and members of Seattle Center’s advisory commission to explore how the festival will look going forward and who will run it. The committee will give the public a chance to weigh in with ideas and there will be a formal request for proposals. Fiscal sustainability will be a primary goal. “Festivals are becoming so much more difficult to deal with and much more difficult to produce and so forth, so we’re going to work with One Reel to help find a new producer for the festival,” Nellams said. “You need a lot of resources to produce festivals going forward and they’re going to help us find somebody that can hopefully take this on for the next few decades, if not longer.” Seattle Center, a city of Seattle department that oversees the area around the Space Needle, announced the decision to reimagine the event on April 23. Griswold called the move bittersweet. One Reel was tapped in February 2020 to help reimagine Bumbershoot for its 50th anniversary later that year, but the pandemic has since taken its toll, reducing the promoter to two employees. The company could no longer realistically plan such a large, multilayered event. “I think all of us were kind of crushed because we really wanted a chance — I can’t stress this enough — to bring back the Bumbershoot that everybody loved and missed over the last several years, and we were really looking forward to doing that,” Griswold said. “We felt like things had really gone off the rails a little bit.”

results.actual_text: Sweden’s chief epidemiologist Anders Tegnell has warned against the strategy of attaining
results.plain_text: Sweden’s chief epidemiologist Anders Tegnell has warned against the strategy of attaining herd immunity in apparent change of heart. In March, Dr Tegnell appeared to ask whether a higher death rate among older people might be acceptable if it led to faster herd immunity. However in comments to German newspaper Die Zeit, Dr Tegnell stated “striving for herd immunity is neither ethical nor otherwise justifiable”. Sweden’s daily coronavirus infections have climbed to a new high while several regions advised residents to avoid public transport and venues to slow the transmission. The Swedish Public Health Agency said that 3254 cases were confirmed in the past 24 hours, a new record since the onset of the pandemic. It was the third record number in a matter of days and came as the country of 10.3 million has registered roughly 121,100 infections and 5934 coronavirus-related deaths in total. Sweden has until recently had “a fairly slow increase (of infections) compared to most other countries,” Dr Tegnell said at a press conference. “We’re beginning to approach the ceiling for what the healthcare system can handle. “Together, as during the spring, we can push down this curve and avoid the strain on healthcare.” Dr Tegnell said the Swedish system of “voluntary measures” had worked well and many people heeded the recommendations. The number of COVID-19 patients in intensive care units (ICU) was still relatively low – about 15 per cent of the current ICU capacity, said Thomas Linden with the National Board of Health and Welfare. Sweden has shunned lockdowns throughout the pandemic. However, several regions – including the most populous areas comprising the main cities of Stockholm and Gothenburg in western Sweden – advised residents to refrain from visits to shopping centres, museums, libraries, swimming pools and fitness centres until November 18. Other advice was for people to avoid meeting with others outside their own household and to work from home where possible, Dr Tegnell said. During the pandemic, Sweden has kept schools and kindergartens open but limited public gatherings to 50 participants, visits in care homes were until recently banned and self service in restaurants and bars suspended. -with agencies
